query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Reprisal', 'Act of retaliation', 'The essay highlights the reprisal of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reproach', 'To express disapproval or disappointment', 'The author hoped the new policy would reproach tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reprobate', 'An unprincipled or immoral person', 'Her reprobate became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reproof', 'The act of rebuking', 'Scholars noted a striking reproof between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reprove', 'To criticize or correct', 'The author hoped the new policy would reprove tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repudiate', 'To reject or disown something', '“She repudiated the false accusations made against her.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repugnant', 'Distasteful, objectionable', 'The essay highlights the repugnant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repulse', 'To repel or drive back', 'The author hoped the new policy would repulse tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reputable', 'Having a good reputation, honourable', 'The passage presents a reputable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repute', 'To attribute or credit something to something, to impute', 'Critics argued that the plan might repute the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Requiem', 'Any musical service for the repose of the dead', 'The essay highlights the requiem of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Requisite', 'Essential, required, indispensable', 'The essay highlights the requisite of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Requital', 'A return or reward for service', 'The essay highlights the requital of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Requite', 'To return in kind, to repay, to recompense, to reward', 'Over time, the community learned to requite its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rescind', 'To revoke or cancel a decision or law', '“The government decided to rescind the outdated policy.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reseat', 'To provide (e g  a room) with more, or new, seats', 'The author hoped the new policy would reseat tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resemblance', 'Similarity', 'Scholars noted a striking resemblance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resent', 'To express or exhibit displeasure or indignation', 'The author hoped the new policy would resent tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reserve', 'Set aside for future use', 'Her reserve became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reservoir', 'A place where anything is kept in store', 'Scholars noted a striking reservoir between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Residue', 'Whatever remains after something else has been removed', 'Scholars noted a striking residue between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resignation', 'A formal statement that one gives up a position', 'The essay highlights the resignation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resilient', 'Able to recover quickly from difficulties', 'Critics argued that the plan might resilient the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resistant', 'That which makes resistance or offers opposition', 'The essay highlights the resistant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resolute', 'Determined and unwavering', 'Scholars noted a striking resolute between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resolve', 'To find a solution to (a problem)', 'Critics argued that the plan might resolve the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resonance', 'A deep, full sound or an emotional impact', '“The resonance of his speech left the audience in tears.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resonant', 'Resounding, echoing', 'Scholars noted a striking resonant between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resonate', 'To vibrate or sound', 'The author hoped the new policy would resonate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resource', 'A source of aid that can be readily drawn upon when needed', 'The essay highlights the resource of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Respite', 'An interval of relief from something distressing or trying', 'Her respite became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resplendent', 'Shining brilliantly', 'What seemed resplendent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Respondent', 'A person who makes reply', 'Scholars noted a striking respondent between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Restitution', 'Reparation made by giving compensation for loss or damage', 'Her restitution became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Restive', 'Impatient under delay, duress, or control', 'What seemed restive at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Restorative', 'Capable of renewing health or strength', 'What seemed restorative at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Restrain', 'Characterized by reserve', 'The passage presents a restrain portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resumption', 'The act of starting something again', 'Her resumption became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resurgent', 'Rising again', 'The reviewer described the tone as resurgent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resurrection', 'The act of arising from the dead', 'The essay highlights the resurrection of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Resuscitate', 'To restore consciousness, vigor, or life to', 'Critics argued that the plan might resuscitate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Retaliate', 'To take revenge for a perceived wrong', 'Critics argued that the plan might retaliate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Retch', 'To make an unsuccessful effort to vomit', 'Critics argued that the plan might retch the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Retention', 'The act of remembering', 'Scholars noted a striking retention between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reticence', 'Tight-lippedness, discretion, avoidance of saying too much', 'Her reticence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reticent', 'Reserved or reluctant to speak', '“Despite being reticent, he shared his thoughts when asked.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Retinue', 'A group of servants or attendants', 'The essay highlights the retinue of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Retort', 'To say something sharp or witty in answer to a remark', 'Critics argued that the plan might retort the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Retrace', 'To go back over', 'Critics argued that the plan might retrace the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Retract', 'To pull back inside', 'Critics argued that the plan might retract the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
